d7b_reasoning: The strategy employs a coherent mean-reversion logic: entering long when price dips below SMA-20 on Mondays with elevated volatility but positive short-term momentum (return_1h > 0.002), then exiting on mean reversion (crosses above SMA-20 with RSI > 58) or profit targets. The dual exit paths (technical reversion vs. time/profit decay after mid-week) show defensible risk management. Theme alignment is strong—day_of_week anchors the entire thesis around Monday open anomalies, a classic calendar effect tied to weekend information gaps and liquidity resumption. However, structural variant risk is high: the factor set {close, day_of_week, realized_vol_24h, return_1h, return_24h, rsi_14, sma_20} extremely closely resembles prior #49 {close, realized_vol_24h, return_1h, return_24h, rsi_14, sma_20} and prior #10 {close, day_of_week, return_1h, return_24h, rsi_14, sma_20}. The current hypothesis simply combines both precedents, adding realized_vol_24h to the day_of_week structure. The novelty lies mainly in threshold choices (day == 0, vol > 0.018, return_1h > 0.002) rather than a genuinely distinct factor composition.
